([British English](/wiki/British_English "British English"): **series finale**; [Australian English](/wiki/Australian_English "Australian English"): **season final**) is the final episode of a [season](/wiki/Television_program#Seasons/series "Television program") of a [television program](/wiki/Television_show "Television show"). This is often the final episode to be produced for a few months or longer, and, as such, will attempt to attract viewers to continue watching when the series begins again.

Travers, Ben (2014-12-04). ["December TV: Why Midseason & Winter Finales Make Shows Better"](https://www.indiewire.com/2014/12/december-tv-why-midseason-winter-finales-make-shows-better-67252/). *IndieWire*. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20191128050639/https://www.indiewire.com/2014/12/december-tv-why-midseason-winter-finales-make-shows-better-67252/) from the original on 2019-11-28. Retrieved 2019-11-28.

["No, Scandal, I will not get excited about your 'winter finale'"](https://www.theguardian.com/tv-and-radio/2014/nov/20/scandal-winter-finale-manipulation-on-demand-tv). *The Guardian*. Retrieved 2019-11-28.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

This article is about the final episode of a television season. For other uses, see [Season finale (disambiguation)](/wiki/Season_finale_(disambiguation) "Season finale (disambiguation)").

Final episode of a television series season

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **This article has multiple issues.** Please help **[improve it](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Season_finale "Special:EditPage/Season finale")** or discuss these issues on the **[talk page](/wiki/Talk:Season_finale "Talk:Season finale")**. *([Learn how and when to remove these messages](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  |  | | --- | --- | | [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Season_finale "Special:EditPage/Season finale") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Season finale"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Season+finale%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Season+finale%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Season+finale%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Season+finale%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Season+finale%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Season+finale%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(May 2018)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  |  |  | | --- | --- | | Globe icon. | The examples and perspective in this article **deal primarily with the United States and do not represent a [worldwide view](/wiki/Wikipedia:WikiProject_Countering_systemic_bias "Wikipedia:WikiProject Countering systemic bias") of the subject**. You may [improve this article](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Season_finale&action=edit), discuss the issue on the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:Season_finale "Talk:Season finale"), or [create a new article](/wiki/Wikipedia:Article_wizard "Wikipedia:Article wizard"), as appropriate. *(May 2018)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

A **season finale** ([British English](/wiki/British_English "British English"): **series finale**; [Australian English](/wiki/Australian_English "Australian English"): **season final**) is the final episode of a [season](/wiki/Television_program#Seasons/series "Television program") of a [television program](/wiki/Television_show "Television show"). This is often the final episode to be produced for a few months or longer, and, as such, will attempt to attract viewers to continue watching when the series begins again.

A season finale may contain a [cliffhanger ending](/wiki/Cliffhanger "Cliffhanger") to be resolved in the next season. Alternatively, a season finale could bring storylines to a close, "going out on a high" and similarly maintaining interest in the series' eventual return.

## Mid-season finale

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Season_finale&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mid-season finale")]

In the 2000s, the terms "mid-season finale", "fall finale" or "winter finale" began being used by television broadcasters in the United States to denote the last episode before a mid-season [hiatus](/wiki/Hiatus_(television) "Hiatus (television)"), often for the [holiday season](/wiki/Christmas_and_holiday_season "Christmas and holiday season"). As with a season finale, a mid-season finale can include a major plot development, or a cliffhanger ending that will be resolved when the series returns. Winter/Fall finales are often used by networks to draw attention and encourage viewership of such episodes as [event television](/wiki/Event_television "Event television"), especially if they fall during the November [sweeps](/wiki/Sweeps "Sweeps") period. The practice has faced criticism for affecting the structure and narrative of broadcast television programs, as writers may be coerced by broadcasters into placing cliffhangers and plot developments in midseason episodes, rather than allow a plot to build up to a traditional season finale.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Series finale

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Season_finale&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Series finale")]

Main article: [Series finale](/wiki/Series_finale "Series finale")

The *final* episode of a television series often concludes the entire premise of the show, wrapping up any loose ends and storylines. On occasion, the season finale has become the series finale due to [cancellation](/wiki/Cancellation_(television) "Cancellation (television)") of the series, sometimes unexpectedly so, [leaving plot points unresolved](/wiki/Unfinished_creative_work#Television "Unfinished creative work").

## In sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Season_finale&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: In sports")]

In [American English](/wiki/American_English "American English"), the term has evolved to describe the final event of a [sporting season](/wiki/Season_(sports) "Season (sports)"), e.g. in [soccer](/wiki/Soccer "Soccer")[[3]](#cite_note-3) or [motocross](/wiki/Motocross "Motocross"),[[4]](#cite_note-4) perhaps partly because of the popularity of these with television viewers.
